workflow_runs.name: Continuous Integration & Verification
workflow_runs.display_title: fix: prevent parallel Chrome instances crashing CI + resilient nav tests

head_commit.message:
fix: prevent parallel Chrome instances crashing CI + resilient nav tests

- Add --test-concurrency=1 to selenium.yml: Node.js 22 runs test files
  in parallel by default (up to availableParallelism() at once). On the
  2-core GitHub Actions runner this launched 4 Chrome instances at the
  same time, exhausting memory and causing 1 random test to fail.
  Sequential execution (concurrency=1) prevents this.

- Upgrade citizen navigation test to use driver.wait(until.urlContains())
  instead of immediate getCurrentUrl() check. React Router's rendering is
  async; an instant URL check can race against it and see the old URL.

workflow_runs.name: Selenium E2E Tests

workflow_runs.name: Continuous Integration & Verification
workflow_runs.display_title: merge: integrate remote main changes with Selenium E2E CI fixes

head_commit.message:
merge: integrate remote main changes with Selenium E2E CI fixes

- Keep fixed selenium.yml (glob fix, Firebase env, selenium-manager)
- Keep fixed firstresponder/package.json (no chromedriver, correct globs)
- Keep remote GlobalState.jsx (has Firebase sync features)
- Keep remote client/functions package-lock.json (up to date)
